calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-13-2013 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-25-2013 Continued To May-13-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ronald Pine and Tim Pupach for Carol De Vincenzi, John Monfredini, and Paul De Vincenzi (Executors). Mark Frisbie for Peter Venturini (Objector). Eugenia Amador (from Mr. Frisbie's office also present) .

Mr. Pupach addressed the issues raised in the Examiner's notes with regard to the verification, receipt, and fees. Mr. Fisbie advised the Court that the case managment conference in the Contra Costa County matter is scheduled for April 23, 2013, that there objection is limited to the note issue only and asked that no determination be made on that issue/asset or on any extraordinary attorney's fees. Mr. Pupach also exlplained that the $200,000.00 note was sold to Lyda Rico de Luca Foundation, that everything has been distributed, that about $750,000.00 in assets remain that all go to Lyda Rico, asked if it was possible to get a waiver of accounting for the $4,000.00 surplus amount and stated that he is going to amend the petition. The Court directed Mr. Frisbie to speak with the probate examiner with regard to the waiver. The accounting petition was continued to May 13, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Nancy Bautista. Bailiff: Deputy Henry.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-20-2013 Continued To Mar-25-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-11-2013 Continued To Mar-20-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Feb-11-2013 Continued To Mar-11-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ronald Pine and Tim Pupach for Executors. Eugenia Amador (present in Court) and Mark Frisbie (appeared by telephone) for Peter Venturini (Objector).

The Court stated that it is not taking any position regarding the $200,000.00 note, that the note is already out of the Estate, that the note has been sold and that the issue of the note is to be addressed on Contra Costa County. The accounting petition was continued to March 11, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Counsel is to address the issues raised in the Probate Examiner's notes with regard to the request for extraordinary fees for the accounting, the Morgan Stanley/Smith Barney account balance, why the $200,000.00 note was added for balancing and the extraordinary services which include litigation services. All of the above noted issues are to be explained in writing at least three days prior to the hearing date.

Judge: Lillian K. Sing. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.
